aiTitle: Design naming convention for RBI package categories

content.content:
129	## Carried forward / consequences
130	
131	- **caparison-w / garrison-w for the `wsl` kind.** The workload's `rbtww-main` is registered via `wsl --import` from a seed tarball; today that seed is created by `wsl --export` of admin's installed distro (GarrisonWsl). Under the `wsl` kind, that export path retires — the seed becomes a verified artifact pulled from GAR. The pre-existing revert pace in ₣A- targeting the WSL-stage DEV CACHE shortcut becomes obsolete and wants dropping or transferring when this heat commits to development.
132	- **Windows onboarding is stale and deferred.** The `rbhw*` Windows handbook tracks (Docker Desktop, context discipline) are stale and incomplete, and `wsl`/`podvm` consumption is deferred — so their onboarding has nothing live to teach. Lean: mark those tracks loudly NOT-available / deferred rather than delete, so the eventual `wsl`-kind consumption work rereads the host-side prose rather than reconstructing it. Onboarding follows *consumption*, not *acquisition*: the Lode acquisition unification does NOT unify the onboarding surface; substrate-kind onboarding lands in the host/Windows tracks, never in the Director build-lifecycle tracks.
133	- **`inscribe` is verb-overloaded — disambiguate, don't conflate.** `rbfl_inscribe` (reliquary tool-mirror, `rbw-dI`) is **in scope** — the operation absorbed into the per-kind capture verbs on the `tool`/`reliquary` kinds (`fetter`/`conclave`). `rbrd_inscribe` (depot tripwire, `rbw-rdi`) is a **different operation, out of scope, stays put**. There is no `mirror` verb to retire: `rbfd_mirror` is the bind vessel-mode copy (made-side, also out of scope). What is absorbed is `inscribe`/reliquary, not `mirror`.
134	- **Onboarding rename surface (tri-surfaced vs greenfield).** `base`/`tool`/`reliquary` are fully present across bash, adoc, and the Director onboarding tracks (`rbw-Odf`/`Oda`/`Odb`) — for these the cost is *rename* (only two typed linked terms, `RBYC_ENSHRINE` + `RBYC_RELIQUARY`, drive the typed references; the rest is hand-edited prose). `wsl`/`podvm-*` are surfaced *nowhere* today — for these the cost is *greenfield*, not rename. Do not estimate the onboarding work as uniform.
135	- **Refresh cadence** is per-kind and may diverge (podvm-wsl vs podvm-native).
136	- **Maintenance verbs stay in the `image` family (`rbw-i*`), deliberately.** `rekon` / `audit` / `wrest` / `jettison` survive intact and do **not** move into `rbw-l*`. They are a low-level backdoor — primitives for cleaning up bad things at the member/tag grain — and that's where they belong; the `l` family is for the first-class Lode lifecycle. Their fetched-side domain variants (Reliquary + Enshrinement) consolidate under Lode/touchmark addressing; the made-side Hallmark variants are out of scope, untouched.
137	- **Tabtarget shape is decided, not open:** per-kind capture verbs under the new `l` (Lode) command family — `rbw-lE/lF/lC/lU/lI` capture, `rbw-lB` banish — superseding the earlier "unified `rbw-dE` with a kind argument" framing. (These move out of the Depot `d` family where `enshrine`/`inscribe`/`yoke` live today.)
138	- **Election (`yoke` + the deferred substrate verb) lives with its consumer, NOT in `l` — lowercase, and does not commit.** Election mutates the *consumer* (vessel for build-time kinds, nameplate for substrate kinds), not the Lode — the touchmark is its *argument*, the consumer its *target* — so it belongs in the consumer's family, paralleling made-side hallmark election (which lives with the nameplate/kludge machinery, never with ark/hallmark). `l` stays reserved for GAR create/destroy of the Lode itself. Three axes separate election from `l`-capture: it targets the **consumer** (not the Lode); it is **lowercase** (cheap reversible config, not persistent GAR/cost state); and it is **operator-committed** — it writes the config and emits a "commit with your usual workflow" hint, never self-committing. That last is the near-universal RBK convention: `yoke` today is explicit — RBSDY, *"this primitive does not commit; the caller commits"* — and the only RBK bash command that self-commits is Marshal-Zero (`rblm`), a wholesale reset ceremony that still gates a clean tree first. Election also does **not self-gate** on a dirty tree; the clean-tree guard lives at the downstream consumer (the build reading the vessel, charge reading the nameplate — `rbob_bottle` already gates the nameplate clean). Pace-time: `yoke` moves out of Depot `d` and de-capitalizes (`rbw-dY` → a lowercase consumer-family colophon); exact destination letter is a layout choice. (Capture verbs raise no git question at all — they write GAR, not git-tracked config.)

## Carried forward / consequences

- **caparison-w / garrison-w for the `wsl` kind.** The workload's `rbtww-main` is registered via `wsl --import` from a seed tarball; today that seed is created by `wsl --export` of admin's installed distro (GarrisonWsl). Under the `wsl` kind, that export path retires — the seed becomes a verified artifact pulled from GAR. The pre-existing revert pace in ₣A- targeting the WSL-stage DEV CACHE shortcut becomes obsolete and wants dropping or transferring when this heat commits to development.
- **Windows onboarding is stale and deferred.** The `rbhw*` Windows handbook tracks (Docker Desktop, context discipline) are stale and incomplete, and `wsl`/`podvm` consumption is deferred — so their onboarding has nothing live to teach. Lean: mark those tracks loudly NOT-available / deferred rather than delete, so the eventual `wsl`-kind consumption work rereads the host-side prose rather than reconstructing it. Onboarding follows *consumption*, not *acquisition*: the Lode acquisition unification does NOT unify the onboarding surface; substrate-kind onboarding lands in the host/Windows tracks, never in the Director build-lifecycle tracks.
- **`inscribe` is verb-overloaded — disambiguate, don't conflate.** `rbfl_inscribe` (reliquary tool-mirror, `rbw-dI`) is **in scope** — the operation absorbed into the per-kind capture verbs on the `tool`/`reliquary` kinds (`fetter`/`conclave`). `rbrd_inscribe` (depot tripwire, `rbw-rdi`) is a **different operation, out of scope, stays put**. There is no `mirror` verb to retire: `rbfd_mirror` is the bind vessel-mode copy (made-side, also out of scope). What is absorbed is `inscribe`/reliquary, not `mirror`.
- **Onboarding rename surface (tri-surfaced vs greenfield).** `base`/`tool`/`reliquary` are fully present across bash, adoc, and the Director onboarding tracks (`rbw-Odf`/`Oda`/`Odb`) — for these the cost is *rename* (only two typed linked terms, `RBYC_ENSHRINE` + `RBYC_RELIQUARY`, drive the typed references; the rest is hand-edited prose). `wsl`/`podvm-*` are surfaced *nowhere* today — for these the cost is *greenfield*, not rename. Do not estimate the onboarding work as uniform.
- **Refresh cadence** is per-kind and may diverge (podvm-wsl vs podvm-native).
- **Maintenance verbs stay in the `image` family (`rbw-i*`), deliberately.** `rekon` / `audit` / `wrest` / `jettison` survive intact and do **not** move into `rbw-l*`. They are a low-level backdoor — primitives for cleaning up bad things at the member/tag grain — and that's where they belong; the `l` family is for the first-class Lode lifecycle. Their fetched-side domain variants (Reliquary + Enshrinement) consolidate under Lode/touchmark addressing; the made-side Hallmark variants are out of scope, untouched.
- **Tabtarget shape is decided, not open:** per-kind capture verbs under the new `l` (Lode) command family — `rbw-lE/lF/lC/lU/lI` capture, `rbw-lB` banish — superseding the earlier "unified `rbw-dE` with a kind argument" framing. (These move out of the Depot `d` family where `enshrine`/`inscribe`/`yoke` live today.)
- **Election (`yoke` + the deferred substrate verb) lives with its consumer, NOT in `l` — lowercase, and does not commit.** Election mutates the *consumer* (vessel for build-time kinds, nameplate for substrate kinds), not the Lode — the touchmark is its *argument*, the consumer its *target* — so it belongs in the consumer's family, paralleling made-side hallmark election (which lives with the nameplate/kludge machinery, never with ark/hallmark). `l` stays reserved for GAR create/destroy of the Lode itself. Three axes separate election from `l`-capture: it targets the **consumer** (not the Lode); it is **lowercase** (cheap reversible config, not persistent GAR/cost state); and it is **operator-committed** — it writes the config and emits a "commit with your usual workflow" hint, never self-committing. That last is the near-universal RBK convention: `yoke` today is explicit — RBSDY, *"this primitive does not commit; the caller commits"* — and the only RBK bash command that self-commits is Marshal-Zero (`rblm`), a wholesale reset ceremony that still gates a clean tree first. Election also does **not self-gate** on a dirty tree; the clean-tree guard lives at the downstream consumer (the build reading the vessel, charge reading the nameplate — `rbob_bottle` already gates the nameplate clean). Pace-time: `yoke` moves out of Depot `d` and de-capitalizes (`rbw-dY` → a lowercase consumer-family colophon); exact destination letter is a layout choice. (Capture verbs raise no git question at all — they write GAR, not git-tracked config.)

The bar forks by kind: `base`/`tool`/`reliquary` carry their consumption-adjacent paths; `wsl`/`podvm` stop at the registry — **capture → list → inspect → per-member + whole-Lode delete against live GAR, no host in the loop** (consumption deferred). Whole-Lode delete is a single `packages delete` — the regression risk shifts to the **per-member delete path** (the cleanup-after-bugs case): multi-member kinds must carry concrete member-absence assertions after a partial delete, since that is where a package can be left in a partial state. file.content:
## Discovery recipes — work to enumerate when paces approach

- **Verb redesign blast radius.** Scan `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSA*.adoc` and `RBSI*.adoc` for verbs touching captured artifacts; for each, classify per-kind variance (unchanged / split / retired). Yoke is the headline because consumer-landing differs per kind; other splits expected. Recipe, not enumeration — premature listing ages poorly. (Quoin anchors live in `RBS0-SpecTop.adoc`: `rbtgo_ark_enshrine`, `rbtgo_depot_inscribe`, `rbtgo_director_yoke`, `gar_enshrines_namespace`/`gar_reliquaries_namespace`, `rbst_reliquary_stamp`, `rbf_fact_reliquary` are the capture cluster.)
- **Substrate election landing.** For `wsl` and `podvm-*`, the touchmark is Director-pinned at the nameplate (determinism premise), materialized at charge — but *which* host-tier regime backs the node-side provisioning (station regime vs BURN node profile vs a new host-config regime) is open. Decide when the first non-vessel Lode consumption pace mounts.
- **Spec letter — `RBSL`** (`L` verified free against the RBS* tree; no `RBSL*` exists). The Lode *code* module must take a prefix **other than `rbl`** (occupied by `rblm` Lifecycle Marshal) to avoid muddying that branch — pace-time pick; colophon `l` and spec `L` don't force it.
- **GAR layout & provenance attachment — DECIDED** (see *Lode registry layout & naming*): single `rbi_ld`, one-package-per-Lode, members + reserved `:rbi_vouch` tag; `rbi_es`/`rbi_rq` migrate into `rbi_ld` at the deferred cutover. The OCI referrers API remains the future upgrade for signed attestation once GAR maturity is confirmed.

## Test coverage gate before deploy

Lifecycle fixtures against the new Lode surface must be slated and landed before deploy: capture / list / inspect / wrest / delete end-to-end against live GAR per kind, bookended by prereq-sweep at head and muster-absent at tail. The bar forks by kind: `base`/`tool`/`reliquary` carry their consumption-adjacent paths; `wsl`/`podvm` stop at the registry — **capture → list → inspect → per-member + whole-Lode delete against live GAR, no host in the loop** (consumption deferred). Whole-Lode delete is a single `packages delete` — the regression risk shifts to the **per-member delete path** (the cleanup-after-bugs case): multi-member kinds must carry concrete member-absence assertions after a partial delete, since that is where a package can be left in a partial state. The fixture surface scales with the operation surface; test paces are not optional polish. Manual operator testing during planning is the bridge, not a substitute.

## Open issues before mounting paces

The Lode *code* module is **`rbld_`** (constants `RBLD_*`, private `zrbld_*`), completing the system-wide Lode naming set: colophon `l` (`rbw-l*`), spec `RBSL`, GAR namespace `rbi_ld`, code `rbld`. (`rbld_` code vs `rbi_ld` namespace are intentionally close — the module produces the namespace.)

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive report:

---

## RBK INTEGRATION RECONNAISSANCE: WIRING POINTS FOR LODE CAPTURE MODULE

### QUESTION 1: MODULE SOURCING & KINDLE HIERARCHY

**Where modules get sourced and kindled:**

The sourcing pattern is **CLI-initiated, hierarchical, dependency-ordered**. Each CLI wrapper (e.g., `rbfd_cli.sh`) is the entry point; it:

1. **Sources constants and base regimes** first
2. **Sources modules in order**, then calls their `z<module>_kindle` functions sequentially

**Template: rbfd_cli.sh sourcing sequence**
- File: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbfd_cli.sh`
- Lines 22–89: The `zrbfd_furnish()` function shows the exact pattern:
  - Source constants: `rbcc_Constants.sh`, `rbgc_Constants.sh`, `rbgl_GarLayout.sh`
  - Source regime files (e.g., `rbrr_regime.sh`, `rbrd_regime.sh`)
  - Source derived constants: `rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh`
  - **Kindle constants first** (lines 66–72): `zrbcc_kindle`, `zrbrr_kindle`, `zrbrd_kindle`, `zrbndb_kindle`
  - **Source modules** (lines 29–50): `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh`, `rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh`, etc.
  - **Kindle modules in dependency order** (lines 76–89): `zrbgc_kindle`, `zrbgl_kindle`, `zrbgd_kindle`, `zrbgo_kindle`, etc., **then** `zrbfd_kindle`

**Kindle function contract:**
- Every module defines `z<module>_kindle()` which:
  - Checks it's not already kindled: `test -z "${Z<MODULE>_KINDLED:-}"`
  - Calls parent module's kindle if needed (e.g., `zrbfd_kindle` calls `zrbfc_kindle`)
  - Initializes readonly state variables and paths
  - Sets `readonly Z<MODULE>_KINDLED=1`

**Template: rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh kindle pattern**
- File: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh`
- Lines 36–93: `zrbfd_kindle()` shows:
  - Sentinel check + parent kindle call
  - Step-by-step initialization of readonly paths and guards
  - Child module kindles (e.g., `zrbfv_kindle` for verify)
  - Final `readonly ZRBFD_KINDLED=1`

**To add Lode module: Create `rbld_Lode.sh`**
- Add module source line to a CLI wrapper (e.g., `rbfd_cli.sh` or a new `rbld_cli.sh`)
- Define `zrbld_kindle()` with standard sentinel + initialization pattern
- Call `zrbld_kindle` in the CLI's furnish function (placed in dependency order after any prerequisites)

---

### QUESTION 2: WORKBENCH ROUTING & ZIPPER ENROLLMENT

**How colophons route to functions:**

The **rbz_zipper.sh** registry is the sole dispatch table. `rbw_workbench.sh` calls `buz_exec_lookup` with the colophon constant; `buz_zipper.sh` (from the shared BURD kit) resolves it to a CLI module + function.

**Template: zipper enrollment (rbz_zipper.sh)**
- File: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbz_zipper.sh`
- Lines 72–84: **Depot family (rbw-d\*)** shows the pattern:
  ```bash
  # Depot — GCP project infrastructure (rbw-d, UPPER=mutates, lower=read)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_DEPOT      "rbw-d"   "Depot — GCP project infrastructure"
  z_mod="rbgp_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_LEVY_DEPOT             "rbw-dL"  "${z_mod}" "rbgp_depot_levy"   ""  "Provision..."
  # ... more enrollments
  z_mod="rbfd_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ENSHRINE_VESSEL        "rbw-dE"  "${z_mod}" "rbfd_enshrine"      "param1"  "Enshrine..."
  z_mod="rbfl_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY     "rbw-dI"  "${z_mod}" "rbfl_inscribe"      ""  "Create reliquary..."
  ```

**Zipper enrollment anatomy:**
- `buz_enroll <VARNAME> <COLOPHON> <CLI_MODULE> <FUNCTION> <CHANNEL> "<DOCSTRING>"`
  - `VARNAME`: exported const naming the colophon (e.g., `RBZ_ENSHRINE_VESSEL`)
  - `COLOPHON`: the human-facing command (e.g., `rbw-dE`)
  - `CLI_MODULE`: which CLI wrapper provides the function (e.g., `rbfd_cli.sh`)
  - `FUNCTION`: the bash function to call (e.g., `rbfd_enshrine`)
  - `CHANNEL`: how args are consumed: `""` = no folio, `"param1"` = first positional arg, `"imprint"` = all args via array (for crucible)
  - `DOCSTRING`: help text

- Add `buz_enroll` calls for each Lode verb (e.g., `rbw-lE` for `ensconce`):
  ```bash

  z_mod="rbld_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ENSCONCE_VESSEL        "rbw-lE"  "${z_mod}" "rbld_ensconce"      "param1"  "Ensconce upstream capture to GAR via Cloud Build"
  ```

### QUESTION 3: TABTARGET CREATION PATTERN

**Tabtarget file layout (tiny launchers):**

Every tabtarget is a 3-line dispatcher that:
1. Sets `BURD_LAUNCHER` to the launcher stub name
2. Execs `z-launcher.sh` with the tabtarget basename + user args

**Template: existing tabtarget (rbw-dE: DirectorEnshrinesVessel)**
- File: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/tt/rbw-dE.DirectorEnshrinesVessel.sh`
  ```bash
  #!/bin/bash
  export BURD_LAUNCHER=launcher.rbw_workbench.sh
  exec "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/z-launcher.sh" "${0##*/}" "${@}"
  ```

**The dispatcher flow:**
1. Tabtarget exports `BURD_LAUNCHER=launcher.rbw_workbench.sh` (the launcher stub that routes all rbw commands)
2. `z-launcher.sh` (lines 1–62 of `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/tt/z-launcher.sh`):
   - Resolves the launcher basename from `${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbml_launchers/${BURD_LAUNCHER}`
   - Normalizes cwd to repo root
   - Execs the launcher with tabtarget basename (e.g., `rbw-dE.DirectorEnshrinesVessel.sh`) + user args
3. Launcher (`rbml_launchers/launcher.rbw_workbench.sh`, shared moorings) execs `rbw_workbench.sh`
4. `rbw_workbench.sh` (lines 54–73):
   - Calls `zrbz_healthcheck` (verifies tabtarget files exist on disk)
   - Routes via `buz_exec_lookup <colophon> <rbk_dir> "$@"` → looks up colophon in the zipper registry

**To add Lode tabtargets, create in `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/tt/`:**
- `rbw-lE.LodeEnsconcesCapture.sh` (or similar naming, matching the colophon):
  ```bash
  #!/bin/bash
  export BURD_LAUNCHER=launcher.rbw_workbench.sh
  exec "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/z-launcher.sh" "${0##*/}" "${@}"
  ```
  (The filename encoding is: `<COLOPHON>.<DescriptiveTitle>.sh`; the colophon is extracted from the filename by the healthcheck, the title is human-facing.)

### QUESTION 4: CLOUD BUILD CAPTURE PIPELINE (ENSHRINE TEMPLATE)

**END-TO-END enshrine path (orchestration → stitch → step → extract):**

**Host-side orchestrator: rbfd_enshrine**
- File: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh`
- Lines 873–928: `rbfd_enshrine()` does:
  1. **Resolve vessel** (sigil or path): `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`
  2. **Load vessel regime**: `zrbfc_load_vessel` (sources rbrv.env, makes vars readonly)
  3. **Validate vessel mode** is "rbnve_conjure" (enshrine only works on conjure vessels with ORIGIN slots)
  4. **Resolve tool images** from reliquary: `zrbfc_resolve_tool_images` (e.g., skopeo)
  5. **Get OAuth token** from Director RBRA: `rbgo_get_token_capture`
  6. **Preflight reliquary** (verify tool images exist): `zrbfd_preflight_reliquary`
  7. **Submit build**: `zrbfd_enshrine_submit` ← orchestrates JSON composition
  8. **Extract anchors**: `zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors` ← reads buildStepOutputs, writes RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR back to rbrv.env

**Build JSON stitching: zrbfd_enshrine_submit**
- Lines 932–1052: Shows the exact stitch pattern:
  1. **Read cloud build step script**: `rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh` (the in-pool executor)
  2. **Compose step JSON** via jq:
     - Name: the tool container (e.g., skopeo image from reliquary)
     - Script: the step body, injected as a `--rawfile` argument to jq
     - ID: human label (e.g., "enshrine-copy")
  3. **Assemble preflight step** (defense-in-depth): `zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step` ← verifies reliquary tool images in-pool before enshrine runs
  4. **Combine steps**: both preflight (step 0) and enshrine-copy (step 1) into a single steps array
  5. **Compose Build resource JSON** via jq `-n` (null input):
     - `steps`: the combined array
     - `substitutions`: maps _<PREFIX>_<VAR> placeholders for the step script
     - `serviceAccount`: Mason SA (ambient auth in-pool for GAR destination)
     - `options.pool`: worker pool (RBDC_POOL_TETHER)
     - `timeout`: build timeout from repo regime (RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT)
  6. **Submit to Cloud Build**: `rbuh_json POST` to the buildAPI

**Substitution variables passed to in-pool step:**
- `_RBGE_GAR_HOST`, `_RBGE_GAR_PATH`: destination registry
- `_RBGE_ENSHRINES_ROOT`, `_RBGE_ENSHRINES_CATEGORY`: package namespace path
- `_RBGE_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN`, `_RBGE_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN`, `_RBGE_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN`: upstream image URIs (or empty strings for unused slots)
- `_RBGR_GAR_HOST`, `_RBGR_GAR_PATH`, `_RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT`, `_RBGR_RELIQUARY`: reliquary preflight parameters
- `_RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n`: (empty placeholders; unused in enshrine)

**In-pool step script: rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh**
- File: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbgje/rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh`
- Lines 1–104: Runs **inside the Cloud Build worker pool**:
  1. **Fetch OAuth token** from metadata server (Mason SA ambient auth):
     ```bash
     TOKEN=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
       "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token")
     ```
  2. **For each non-empty ORIGIN slot (1, 2, 3):**
     - `skopeo inspect --raw`: get upstream manifest (digest)
     - Compute SHA256 of raw manifest
     - Construct **anchor**: sanitize ORIGIN (`:` and `/` → `-`), append first 10 hex of digest
     - Construct **locator**: `<CATEGORY>/<ANCHOR>:<ANCHOR>` (writeback target for rbrv.env)
     - `skopeo copy --all`: copy upstream to GAR with anchor tag
     - **Write JSON result**: `"slot_<n>": {"anchor": "<LOCATOR>", ...}`
  3. **Write results to buildStepOutputs**: `/builder/outputs/output` (plain JSON, will be base64-encoded by Cloud Build)

**Output extraction: zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors**
- Lines 1058–1122: Runs **on the host** after build completes:
  1. **Extract base64 from buildStepOutputs[1]**: the enshrine-copy step's output (step 0 is preflight, has no output)
  2. **Decode base64** to JSON
  3. **For each slot n (1, 2, 3):**
     - Extract `.slot_n.anchor` (the locator)
     - Update/append `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR=<locator>` in the vessel's rbrv.env
     - Commit rbrv.env back to disk

**Shared vs. enshrine-specific infrastructure:**

| Category | Scope | Shared? | Template Files |
|----------|-------|---------|-----------------|
| Vessel resolution/loading | core | Shared (rbfc_*) | `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`, `zrbfc_load_vessel` in `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` |
| Tool image resolution | GAR layout | Shared | `zrbfc_resolve_tool_images` |
| OAuth token retrieval | auth | Shared | `rbgo_get_token_capture` in `rbgo_OAuth.sh` |
| Preflight (in-pool reliquary check) | assembly | Shared | `zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step` in `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` |
| Step stitching (jq composition) | build | Shared | `zrbfc_assemble_*_step` patterns |
| Cloud Build submission | API | Shared | `rbuh_json POST` in `rbuh_Http.sh` |
| buildStepOutputs extraction | parse | **Enshrine-specific** | `zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors` |
| Anchor computation | **capture-specific** | **NEW (copy to ensconce)** | `rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh` logic |
| rbrv.env writeback | **enshrine-specific** | **NEW (copy to ensconce)** | lines 1090–1122 of `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` |

- Create **`rbgje/rbgje0X-lode-ensconce.sh`** in-pool step (copy/adapt `rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh` with capture-specific logic)

**Cloud Build environment (tools available in the pool):**

### QUESTION 5: THEURGE TEST FIXTURE FRAMEWORK

**Fixture definition & registration:**

Fixtures are Rust static structs defined in `rbtd/src/*.rs` files and registered in a global fixtures array.

**Fast fixture template: enrollment-validation (no external deps)**
- Definition location: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_fast.rs`
- Lines 1873–1995: Shows the pattern:
  ```rust
  // Case array: list of test case functions
  pub static RBTDRF_CASES_ENROLLMENT_VALIDATION: &[rbtdre_Case] = &[
      case!(rbtdrf_ev_string_valid),
      case!(rbtdrf_ev_string_empty_optional),
      // ... more cases ...
  ];

  // Fixture: groups cases with metadata
  pub static RBTDRF_FIXTURE_ENROLLMENT_VALIDATION: rbtdre_Fixture = rbtdre_Fixture {
      name: RBTDRM_FIXTURE_ENROLLMENT_VALIDATION,  // const from manifest
      disposition: rbtdre_Disposition::Independent, // no setup/teardown or state carryover
      setup: None,
      teardown: None,
      cases: RBTDRF_CASES_ENROLLMENT_VALIDATION,
  };
  ```

- Each case is a function that returns `rbtdre_Verdict::Pass` or `rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(msg)`:
  ```rust
  fn rbtdrf_ev_string_valid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
      rbtdrf_run_ev(dir,
          "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\n...",  // setup: regime enrollment shell snippet
          &[RbtdrfSub::ok("valid strings",
              "export TEST_NAME=\"hello\"\n...")],  // sub-assertions
      )
  }
  ```

**Service fixture template: hallmark-lifecycle (live GCP, independent)**
- Definition location: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs`
- Lines 2330–2336: Shows the pattern (service fixtures are also Independent unless they need setup/teardown):

  ```rust
      name: crate::rbtdrm_manifest::RBTDRM_FIXTURE_HALLMARK_LIFECYCLE,
      disposition: rbtdre_Disposition::Independent,  // each case is self-contained
      setup: None,
      teardown: None,
      cases: RBTDRC_CASES_HALLMARK_LIFECYCLE,
  };
  ```

- Case functions invoke tabtargets directly and assert on exit codes:
  ```rust
  fn rbtdrc_hallmark_lifecycle(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
      rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
          // Invoke tabtarget via colophon
          rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_ORDAIN_HALLMARK, &["vessel_sigil"])?;
          // Assert exit code
          // Read back state facts via rbtdri_read_burv_fact()
          // Continue with next step...
      })
  }
  ```

**Colophon manifest: required-colophons mapping**
- File: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs`
- Lines 135–257: `rbtdrm_required_colophons(fixture: &str)` returns the set of colophons a fixture exercises
  - Example (lines 148–159): hallmark-lifecycle requires `&[ORDAIN_HALLMARK, ABJURE_HALLMARK, REKON_HALLMARK, AUDIT_HALLMARKS]`
  - The healthcheck in `rbw_workbench.sh` verifies each colophon's tabtarget file exists on disk
  - Fast fixtures like enrollment-validation return `Some(&[])` (no external colophons)

**Suite enrollment (fast vs. service):**

Suites partition fixtures by execution context. Fixtures register themselves in the global FIXTURES array (line 2358–2383 of `rbtdrc_crucible.rs`):

```rust
pub static RBTDRC_FIXTURES: &[&'static rbtdre_Fixture] = &[
    // Crucible fixtures (require charged runtime)
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_TADMOR,
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_MORIAH,
    // Bare fixtures (live GCP, no container)
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_HALLMARK_LIFECYCLE,
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_BATCH_VOUCH,
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_ACCESS_PROBE,
    // Fast fixtures (deterministic, no external deps)
    &crate::rbtdrf_fast::RBTDRF_FIXTURE_ENROLLMENT_VALIDATION,
    &crate::rbtdrf_fast::RBTDRF_FIXTURE_REGIME_VALIDATION,
    // ... etc
];
```

The **theurge engine** reads this array at runtime via `rbtdrc_lookup_fixture(name)` and dispatches to the correct fixture. Suites are defined in `rbtdre_engine.rs` (the core invocation harness) and are logical groupings — at runtime, a suite name (e.g., `"fast"`) maps to a list of fixture names via code logic, not a separate registry.

**To add a new fast fixture (e.g., lode-capture-validate):**

1. **Create fixture name const** in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`:
   ```rust
   pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_LODE_CAPTURE_VALIDATE: &str = "lode-capture-validate";
   ```

2. **Add required-colophons mapping** in `rbtdrm_required_colophons()`:
   ```rust
   RBTDRM_FIXTURE_LODE_CAPTURE_VALIDATE => Some(&[]),  // no colophons if it's pure regime validation
   ```

3. **Define case functions** in a new file `rbtdlc_lode_capture.rs` (or in `rbtdrf_fast.rs` if bundled with other fast tests):
   ```rust
   fn rbtdlc_capture_envelope_valid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
       rbtdrf_run_probate(dir, "rbld_Lode.sh", "rbld_probate",
           "export RBLD_ENVELOPE_TYPE=\"canonical\"\n...",
           "",
           true,
           "envelope envelope envelope")
   }
   ```

4. **Define cases array** and **fixture static**:
   ```rust
   pub static RBTDLC_CASES_CAPTURE_VALIDATE: &[rbtdre_Case] = &[
       case!(rbtdlc_capture_envelope_valid),
       case!(rbtdlc_capture_resolution_valid),
       // ...
   ];

   pub static RBTDLC_FIXTURE_CAPTURE_VALIDATE: rbtdre_Fixture = rbtdre_Fixture {
       name: RBTDRM_FIXTURE_LODE_CAPTURE_VALIDATE,
       disposition: rbtdre_Disposition::Independent,
       setup: None,
       teardown: None,
       cases: RBTDLC_CASES_CAPTURE_VALIDATE,
   };
   ```

5. **Register in FIXTURES array** (in the file where fixtures are declared, e.g., `rbtdrf_fast.rs` or new `rbtdlc_lode_capture.rs`):
   ```rust
   pub static RBTDRC_FIXTURES: &[&'static rbtdre_Fixture] = &[
       // ... existing fixtures ...
       &crate::rbtdlc_lode_capture::RBTDLC_FIXTURE_CAPTURE_VALIDATE,
       // ...
   ];
   ```

**To add a new service fixture (e.g., lode-capture-lifecycle, live-GAR capture→list→inspect→delete):**

1. **Create fixture name const** in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`:
   ```rust
   pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_LODE_CAPTURE_LIFECYCLE: &str = "lode-capture-lifecycle";
   ```

2. **Add required-colophons mapping** (this fixture will exercise ensconce and related colophons):
   ```rust
   RBTDRM_FIXTURE_LODE_CAPTURE_LIFECYCLE => Some(&[
       RBTDGC_ENSCONCE_VESSEL,
       RBTDGC_AUDIT_CAPTURES,       // or custom colophons for list/inspect
       RBTDGC_JETTISON_CAPTURE,     // or custom colophon for delete
   ]),
   ```

3. **Define case functions** in a new file `rbtdll_lode_lifecycle.rs`:
   ```rust
   fn rbtdll_capture_roundtrip(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
       rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
           // Step 1: Ensconce a vessel (captures upstream to GAR)
           rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_ENSCONCE_VESSEL, &["vessel_sigil"])?;
           // Step 2: List captures (verify artifact appeared)
           let list_result = rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_AUDIT_CAPTURES, &[])?;
           // Assert capture is in the list...
           // Step 3: Inspect capture (metadata)
           let inspect = rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_INSPECT_CAPTURE, &["capture_id"])?;
           // Assert metadata contains expected fields...
           // Step 4: Delete capture (cleanup)
           rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_JETTISON_CAPTURE, &["capture_id"])?;
           // Assert capture no longer in list...
       })
   }
   ```

4. **Disposition: Dependent (if setup/teardown required)**:
   ```rust
   pub static RBTDLL_FIXTURE_CAPTURE_LIFECYCLE: rbtdre_Fixture = rbtdre_Fixture {
       name: RBTDRM_FIXTURE_LODE_CAPTURE_LIFECYCLE,
       disposition: rbtdre_Disposition::Dependent,  // requires per-case state (depot, etc.)
       setup: Some(rbtdll_setup_depot),             // provision GCP depot once per fixture
       teardown: Some(rbtdll_teardown_depot),       // clean up depot after all cases
       cases: RBTDLL_CASES_CAPTURE_LIFECYCLE,
   };

   fn rbtdll_setup_depot(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context) -> Result<(), String> {
       rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_LEVY_DEPOT, &[])
           .map(|r| if r.exit_code == 0 { Ok(()) } else { Err(format!("Levy failed: {}", r.stderr)) })?
   }
   ```

5. **Register in FIXTURES array** and wire into suite logic (where suite → fixture mapping happens, typically in engine or manifest init code)

**Fixture discovery: the `case!()` macro**
- Defined in the crate root (lib.rs or macros): `case!(function_name)` wraps a function pointer into an `rbtdre_Case` struct with metadata (name, function pointer)
- The engine calls `case_fn(&dir)` where `dir` is a temp directory scoped to the case for artifacts/logs

## RBK INTEGRATION RECONNAISSANCE: WIRING POINTS FOR LODE CAPTURE MODULE

### QUESTION 1: MODULE SOURCING & KINDLE HIERARCHY

**Where modules get sourced and kindled:**

The sourcing pattern is **CLI-initiated, hierarchical, dependency-ordered**. Each CLI wrapper (e.g., `rbfd_cli.sh`) is the entry point; it:

1. **Sources constants and base regimes** first
2. **Sources modules in order**, then calls their `z<module>_kindle` functions sequentially

**Template: rbfd_cli.sh sourcing sequence**
- File: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbfd_cli.sh`
- Lines 22–89: The `zrbfd_furnish()` function shows the exact pattern:
  - Source constants: `rbcc_Constants.sh`, `rbgc_Constants.sh`, `rbgl_GarLayout.sh`
  - Source regime files (e.g., `rbrr_regime.sh`, `rbrd_regime.sh`)
  - Source derived constants: `rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh`
  - **Kindle constants first** (lines 66–72): `zrbcc_kindle`, `zrbrr_kindle`, `zrbrd_kindle`, `zrbndb_kindle`
  - **Source modules** (lines 29–50): `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh`, `rbfh_FoundryHygiene.sh`, etc.
  - **Kindle modules in dependency order** (lines 76–89): `zrbgc_kindle`, `zrbgl_kindle`, `zrbgd_kindle`, `zrbgo_kindle`, etc., **then** `zrbfd_kindle`

**Kindle function contract:**
- Every module defines `z<module>_kindle()` which:
  - Checks it's not already kindled: `test -z "${Z<MODULE>_KINDLED:-}"`
  - Calls parent module's kindle if needed (e.g., `zrbfd_kindle` calls `zrbfc_kindle`)
  - Initializes readonly state variables and paths
  - Sets `readonly Z<MODULE>_KINDLED=1`

**Template: rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh kindle pattern**
- File: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh`
- Lines 36–93: `zrbfd_kindle()` shows:
  - Sentinel check + parent kindle call
  - Step-by-step initialization of readonly paths and guards
  - Child module kindles (e.g., `zrbfv_kindle` for verify)
  - Final `readonly ZRBFD_KINDLED=1`

**To add Lode module: Create `rbld_Lode.sh`**
- Add module source line to a CLI wrapper (e.g., `rbfd_cli.sh` or a new `rbld_cli.sh`)
- Define `zrbld_kindle()` with standard sentinel + initialization pattern
- Call `zrbld_kindle` in the CLI's furnish function (placed in dependency order after any prerequisites)

---

### QUESTION 2: WORKBENCH ROUTING & ZIPPER ENROLLMENT

**How colophons route to functions:**

The **rbz_zipper.sh** registry is the sole dispatch table. `rbw_workbench.sh` calls `buz_exec_lookup` with the colophon constant; `buz_zipper.sh` (from the shared BURD kit) resolves it to a CLI module + function.

**Template: zipper enrollment (rbz_zipper.sh)**
- File: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbz_zipper.sh`
- Lines 72–84: **Depot family (rbw-d\*)** shows the pattern:
  ```bash
  # Depot — GCP project infrastructure (rbw-d, UPPER=mutates, lower=read)
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_DEPOT      "rbw-d"   "Depot — GCP project infrastructure"
  z_mod="rbgp_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_LEVY_DEPOT             "rbw-dL"  "${z_mod}" "rbgp_depot_levy"   ""  "Provision..."
  # ... more enrollments
  z_mod="rbfd_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ENSHRINE_VESSEL        "rbw-dE"  "${z_mod}" "rbfd_enshrine"      "param1"  "Enshrine..."
  z_mod="rbfl_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY     "rbw-dI"  "${z_mod}" "rbfl_inscribe"      ""  "Create reliquary..."
  ```

**Zipper enrollment anatomy:**
- `buz_enroll <VARNAME> <COLOPHON> <CLI_MODULE> <FUNCTION> <CHANNEL> "<DOCSTRING>"`
  - `VARNAME`: exported const naming the colophon (e.g., `RBZ_ENSHRINE_VESSEL`)
  - `COLOPHON`: the human-facing command (e.g., `rbw-dE`)
  - `CLI_MODULE`: which CLI wrapper provides the function (e.g., `rbfd_cli.sh`)
  - `FUNCTION`: the bash function to call (e.g., `rbfd_enshrine`)
  - `CHANNEL`: how args are consumed: `""` = no folio, `"param1"` = first positional arg, `"imprint"` = all args via array (for crucible)
  - `DOCSTRING`: help text

**To add Lode colophon family (rbw-l\*), in rbz_zipper.sh:**
- Add a `buz_group` block for the Lode family (around line 72–84)
- Add `buz_enroll` calls for each Lode verb (e.g., `rbw-lE` for `ensconce`):
  ```bash
  buz_group RBZ__GROUP_LODE       "rbw-l"   "Lode — Capture subsystem for GAR artifacts"
  z_mod="rbld_cli.sh"
  buz_enroll RBZ_ENSCONCE_VESSEL        "rbw-lE"  "${z_mod}" "rbld_ensconce"      "param1"  "Ensconce upstream capture to GAR via Cloud Build"
  ```
- Inside the `zrbz_kindle()` function (which auto-calls `buz_enroll` in order), ensure the new enrollments are declared before `readonly ZRBZ_KINDLED=1` (line 222)

---

### QUESTION 3: TABTARGET CREATION PATTERN

**Tabtarget file layout (tiny launchers):**

Every tabtarget is a 3-line dispatcher that:
1. Sets `BURD_LAUNCHER` to the launcher stub name
2. Execs `z-launcher.sh` with the tabtarget basename + user args

**Template: existing tabtarget (rbw-dE: DirectorEnshrinesVessel)**
- File: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/tt/rbw-dE.DirectorEnshrinesVessel.sh`
  ```bash
  #!/bin/bash
  export BURD_LAUNCHER=launcher.rbw_workbench.sh
  exec "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/z-launcher.sh" "${0##*/}" "${@}"
  ```

**The dispatcher flow:**
1. Tabtarget exports `BURD_LAUNCHER=launcher.rbw_workbench.sh` (the launcher stub that routes all rbw commands)
2. `z-launcher.sh` (lines 1–62 of `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/tt/z-launcher.sh`):
   - Resolves the launcher basename from `${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbml_launchers/${BURD_LAUNCHER}`
   - Normalizes cwd to repo root
   - Execs the launcher with tabtarget basename (e.g., `rbw-dE.DirectorEnshrinesVessel.sh`) + user args
3. Launcher (`rbml_launchers/launcher.rbw_workbench.sh`, shared moorings) execs `rbw_workbench.sh`
4. `rbw_workbench.sh` (lines 54–73):
   - Calls `zrbz_healthcheck` (verifies tabtarget files exist on disk)
   - Routes via `buz_exec_lookup <colophon> <rbk_dir> "$@"` → looks up colophon in the zipper registry

**To add Lode tabtargets, create in `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/tt/`:**
- `rbw-lE.LodeEnsconcesCapture.sh` (or similar naming, matching the colophon):
  ```bash
  #!/bin/bash
  export BURD_LAUNCHER=launcher.rbw_workbench.sh
  exec "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/z-launcher.sh" "${0##*/}" "${@}"
  ```
  (The filename encoding is: `<COLOPHON>.<DescriptiveTitle>.sh`; the colophon is extracted from the filename by the healthcheck, the title is human-facing.)

---

### QUESTION 4: CLOUD BUILD CAPTURE PIPELINE (ENSHRINE TEMPLATE)

**END-TO-END enshrine path (orchestration → stitch → step → extract):**

**Host-side orchestrator: rbfd_enshrine**
- File: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh`
- Lines 873–928: `rbfd_enshrine()` does:
  1. **Resolve vessel** (sigil or path): `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`
  2. **Load vessel regime**: `zrbfc_load_vessel` (sources rbrv.env, makes vars readonly)
  3. **Validate vessel mode** is "rbnve_conjure" (enshrine only works on conjure vessels with ORIGIN slots)
  4. **Resolve tool images** from reliquary: `zrbfc_resolve_tool_images` (e.g., skopeo)
  5. **Get OAuth token** from Director RBRA: `rbgo_get_token_capture`
  6. **Preflight reliquary** (verify tool images exist): `zrbfd_preflight_reliquary`
  7. **Submit build**: `zrbfd_enshrine_submit` ← orchestrates JSON composition
  8. **Extract anchors**: `zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors` ← reads buildStepOutputs, writes RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR back to rbrv.env

**Build JSON stitching: zrbfd_enshrine_submit**
- Lines 932–1052: Shows the exact stitch pattern:
  1. **Read cloud build step script**: `rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh` (the in-pool executor)
  2. **Compose step JSON** via jq:
     - Name: the tool container (e.g., skopeo image from reliquary)
     - Script: the step body, injected as a `--rawfile` argument to jq
     - ID: human label (e.g., "enshrine-copy")
  3. **Assemble preflight step** (defense-in-depth): `zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step` ← verifies reliquary tool images in-pool before enshrine runs
  4. **Combine steps**: both preflight (step 0) and enshrine-copy (step 1) into a single steps array
  5. **Compose Build resource JSON** via jq `-n` (null input):
     - `steps`: the combined array
     - `substitutions`: maps _<PREFIX>_<VAR> placeholders for the step script
     - `serviceAccount`: Mason SA (ambient auth in-pool for GAR destination)
     - `options.pool`: worker pool (RBDC_POOL_TETHER)
     - `timeout`: build timeout from repo regime (RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT)
  6. **Submit to Cloud Build**: `rbuh_json POST` to the buildAPI

**Substitution variables passed to in-pool step:**
- `_RBGE_GAR_HOST`, `_RBGE_GAR_PATH`: destination registry
- `_RBGE_ENSHRINES_ROOT`, `_RBGE_ENSHRINES_CATEGORY`: package namespace path
- `_RBGE_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN`, `_RBGE_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN`, `_RBGE_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN`: upstream image URIs (or empty strings for unused slots)
- `_RBGR_GAR_HOST`, `_RBGR_GAR_PATH`, `_RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT`, `_RBGR_RELIQUARY`: reliquary preflight parameters
- `_RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n`: (empty placeholders; unused in enshrine)

**In-pool step script: rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh**
- File: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbgje/rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh`
- Lines 1–104: Runs **inside the Cloud Build worker pool**:
  1. **Fetch OAuth token** from metadata server (Mason SA ambient auth):
     ```bash
     TOKEN=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
       "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token")
     ```
  2. **For each non-empty ORIGIN slot (1, 2, 3):**
     - `skopeo inspect --raw`: get upstream manifest (digest)
     - Compute SHA256 of raw manifest
     - Construct **anchor**: sanitize ORIGIN (`:` and `/` → `-`), append first 10 hex of digest
     - Construct **locator**: `<CATEGORY>/<ANCHOR>:<ANCHOR>` (writeback target for rbrv.env)
     - `skopeo copy --all`: copy upstream to GAR with anchor tag
     - **Write JSON result**: `"slot_<n>": {"anchor": "<LOCATOR>", ...}`
  3. **Write results to buildStepOutputs**: `/builder/outputs/output` (plain JSON, will be base64-encoded by Cloud Build)

**Output extraction: zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors**
- Lines 1058–1122: Runs **on the host** after build completes:
  1. **Extract base64 from buildStepOutputs[1]**: the enshrine-copy step's output (step 0 is preflight, has no output)
  2. **Decode base64** to JSON
  3. **For each slot n (1, 2, 3):**
     - Extract `.slot_n.anchor` (the locator)
     - Update/append `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR=<locator>` in the vessel's rbrv.env
     - Commit rbrv.env back to disk

**Shared vs. enshrine-specific infrastructure:**

| Category | Scope | Shared? | Template Files |
|----------|-------|---------|-----------------|
| Vessel resolution/loading | core | Shared (rbfc_*) | `zrbfc_resolve_vessel`, `zrbfc_load_vessel` in `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` |
| Tool image resolution | GAR layout | Shared | `zrbfc_resolve_tool_images` |
| OAuth token retrieval | auth | Shared | `rbgo_get_token_capture` in `rbgo_OAuth.sh` |
| Preflight (in-pool reliquary check) | assembly | Shared | `zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step` in `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` |
| Step stitching (jq composition) | build | Shared | `zrbfc_assemble_*_step` patterns |
| Cloud Build submission | API | Shared | `rbuh_json POST` in `rbuh_Http.sh` |
| buildStepOutputs extraction | parse | **Enshrine-specific** | `zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors` |
| Anchor computation | **capture-specific** | **NEW (copy to ensconce)** | `rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh` logic |
| rbrv.env writeback | **enshrine-specific** | **NEW (copy to ensconce)** | lines 1090–1122 of `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` |

**To add Lode ensconce subsystem:**
- Create **`rbld_Lode.sh`** module with `rbld_ensconce()` orchestrator (copy/adapt `rbfd_enshrine`)
- Create **`rbgje/rbgje0X-lode-ensconce.sh`** in-pool step (copy/adapt `rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh` with capture-specific logic)
- Create **`zrbld_ensconce_submit()`** (copy `zrbfd_enshrine_submit` and adapt substitutions/step name)
- Create **`zrbld_ensconce_extract_anchors()`** (copy `zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors` and adapt result field mapping if different)
- Add `zrbld_kindle()` to initialize paths and child module kindles
- Source `rbld_Lode.sh` in a CLI wrapper (e.g., `rbld_cli.sh` or add to `rbfd_cli.sh` if bundled with enshrine)
- Enroll `rbw-lE` colophon in `rbz_zipper.sh` via `buz_enroll RBZ_ENSCONCE_VESSEL "rbw-lE" "rbld_cli.sh" "rbld_ensconce" "param1"`

**Cloud Build environment (tools available in the pool):**
- **Container runtime**: the reliquary-provided tool image specified in the step (`name: $zjq_tool_name`)
- **Ambient auth**: Mason SA via metadata server (standard GCP Cloud Build ambient auth)
- **Available tools in skopeo container**: `skopeo` itself, `sha256sum` (coreutils), `curl`, `grep`, `cut`, bash, jq (if the container includes it; enshrine step doesn't use jq)
- **Artifact upload**: via `oauth2accesstoken:<TOKEN>` credentials in skopeo copy `--dest-creds`

---

### QUESTION 5: THEURGE TEST FIXTURE FRAMEWORK

**Fixture definition & registration:**

Fixtures are Rust static structs defined in `rbtd/src/*.rs` files and registered in a global fixtures array.

**Fast fixture template: enrollment-validation (no external deps)**
- Definition location: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_fast.rs`
- Lines 1873–1995: Shows the pattern:
  ```rust
  // Case array: list of test case functions
  pub static RBTDRF_CASES_ENROLLMENT_VALIDATION: &[rbtdre_Case] = &[
      case!(rbtdrf_ev_string_valid),
      case!(rbtdrf_ev_string_empty_optional),
      // ... more cases ...
  ];

  // Fixture: groups cases with metadata
  pub static RBTDRF_FIXTURE_ENROLLMENT_VALIDATION: rbtdre_Fixture = rbtdre_Fixture {
      name: RBTDRM_FIXTURE_ENROLLMENT_VALIDATION,  // const from manifest
      disposition: rbtdre_Disposition::Independent, // no setup/teardown or state carryover
      setup: None,
      teardown: None,
      cases: RBTDRF_CASES_ENROLLMENT_VALIDATION,
  };
  ```

- Each case is a function that returns `rbtdre_Verdict::Pass` or `rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(msg)`:
  ```rust
  fn rbtdrf_ev_string_valid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
      rbtdrf_run_ev(dir,
          "buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\n...",  // setup: regime enrollment shell snippet
          &[RbtdrfSub::ok("valid strings",
              "export TEST_NAME=\"hello\"\n...")],  // sub-assertions
      )
  }
  ```

**Service fixture template: hallmark-lifecycle (live GCP, independent)**
- Definition location: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs`
- Lines 2330–2336: Shows the pattern (service fixtures are also Independent unless they need setup/teardown):
  ```rust
  pub static RBTDRC_FIXTURE_HALLMARK_LIFECYCLE: rbtdre_Fixture = rbtdre_Fixture {
      name: crate::rbtdrm_manifest::RBTDRM_FIXTURE_HALLMARK_LIFECYCLE,
      disposition: rbtdre_Disposition::Independent,  // each case is self-contained
      setup: None,
      teardown: None,
      cases: RBTDRC_CASES_HALLMARK_LIFECYCLE,
  };
  ```

- Case functions invoke tabtargets directly and assert on exit codes:
  ```rust
  fn rbtdrc_hallmark_lifecycle(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
      rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
          // Invoke tabtarget via colophon
          rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_ORDAIN_HALLMARK, &["vessel_sigil"])?;
          // Assert exit code
          // Read back state facts via rbtdri_read_burv_fact()
          // Continue with next step...
      })
  }
  ```

**Colophon manifest: required-colophons mapping**
- File: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs`
- Lines 135–257: `rbtdrm_required_colophons(fixture: &str)` returns the set of colophons a fixture exercises
  - Example (lines 148–159): hallmark-lifecycle requires `&[ORDAIN_HALLMARK, ABJURE_HALLMARK, REKON_HALLMARK, AUDIT_HALLMARKS]`
  - The healthcheck in `rbw_workbench.sh` verifies each colophon's tabtarget file exists on disk
  - Fast fixtures like enrollment-validation return `Some(&[])` (no external colophons)

**Suite enrollment (fast vs. service):**

Suites partition fixtures by execution context. Fixtures register themselves in the global FIXTURES array (line 2358–2383 of `rbtdrc_crucible.rs`):

```rust
pub static RBTDRC_FIXTURES: &[&'static rbtdre_Fixture] = &[
    // Crucible fixtures (require charged runtime)
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_TADMOR,
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_MORIAH,
    // Bare fixtures (live GCP, no container)
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_HALLMARK_LIFECYCLE,
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_BATCH_VOUCH,
    &RBTDRC_FIXTURE_ACCESS_PROBE,
    // Fast fixtures (deterministic, no external deps)
    &crate::rbtdrf_fast::RBTDRF_FIXTURE_ENROLLMENT_VALIDATION,
    &crate::rbtdrf_fast::RBTDRF_FIXTURE_REGIME_VALIDATION,
    // ... etc
];
```

The **theurge engine** reads this array at runtime via `rbtdrc_lookup_fixture(name)` and dispatches to the correct fixture. Suites are defined in `rbtdre_engine.rs` (the core invocation harness) and are logical groupings — at runtime, a suite name (e.g., `"fast"`) maps to a list of fixture names via code logic, not a separate registry.

**To add a new fast fixture (e.g., lode-capture-validate):**

1. **Create fixture name const** in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`:
   ```rust
   pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_LODE_CAPTURE_VALIDATE: &str = "lode-capture-validate";
   ```

2. **Add required-colophons mapping** in `rbtdrm_required_colophons()`:
   ```rust
   RBTDRM_FIXTURE_LODE_CAPTURE_VALIDATE => Some(&[]),  // no colophons if it's pure regime validation
   ```

3. **Define case functions** in a new file `rbtdlc_lode_capture.rs` (or in `rbtdrf_fast.rs` if bundled with other fast tests):
   ```rust
   fn rbtdlc_capture_envelope_valid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
       rbtdrf_run_probate(dir, "rbld_Lode.sh", "rbld_probate",
           "export RBLD_ENVELOPE_TYPE=\"canonical\"\n...",
           "",
           true,
           "envelope envelope envelope")
   }
   ```

4. **Define cases array** and **fixture static**:
   ```rust
   pub static RBTDLC_CASES_CAPTURE_VALIDATE: &[rbtdre_Case] = &[
       case!(rbtdlc_capture_envelope_valid),
       case!(rbtdlc_capture_resolution_valid),
       // ...
   ];

   pub static RBTDLC_FIXTURE_CAPTURE_VALIDATE: rbtdre_Fixture = rbtdre_Fixture {
       name: RBTDRM_FIXTURE_LODE_CAPTURE_VALIDATE,
       disposition: rbtdre_Disposition::Independent,
       setup: None,
       teardown: None,
       cases: RBTDLC_CASES_CAPTURE_VALIDATE,
   };
   ```

5. **Register in FIXTURES array** (in the file where fixtures are declared, e.g., `rbtdrf_fast.rs` or new `rbtdlc_lode_capture.rs`):
   ```rust
   pub static RBTDRC_FIXTURES: &[&'static rbtdre_Fixture] = &[
       // ... existing fixtures ...
       &crate::rbtdlc_lode_capture::RBTDLC_FIXTURE_CAPTURE_VALIDATE,
       // ...
   ];
   ```

**To add a new service fixture (e.g., lode-capture-lifecycle, live-GAR capture→list→inspect→delete):**

1. **Create fixture name const** in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`:
   ```rust
   pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_LODE_CAPTURE_LIFECYCLE: &str = "lode-capture-lifecycle";
   ```

2. **Add required-colophons mapping** (this fixture will exercise ensconce and related colophons):
   ```rust
   RBTDRM_FIXTURE_LODE_CAPTURE_LIFECYCLE => Some(&[
       RBTDGC_ENSCONCE_VESSEL,
       RBTDGC_AUDIT_CAPTURES,       // or custom colophons for list/inspect
       RBTDGC_JETTISON_CAPTURE,     // or custom colophon for delete
   ]),
   ```

3. **Define case functions** in a new file `rbtdll_lode_lifecycle.rs`:
   ```rust
   fn rbtdll_capture_roundtrip(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
       rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| {
           // Step 1: Ensconce a vessel (captures upstream to GAR)
           rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_ENSCONCE_VESSEL, &["vessel_sigil"])?;
           // Step 2: List captures (verify artifact appeared)
           let list_result = rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_AUDIT_CAPTURES, &[])?;
           // Assert capture is in the list...
           // Step 3: Inspect capture (metadata)
           let inspect = rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_INSPECT_CAPTURE, &["capture_id"])?;
           // Assert metadata contains expected fields...
           // Step 4: Delete capture (cleanup)
           rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_JETTISON_CAPTURE, &["capture_id"])?;
           // Assert capture no longer in list...
       })
   }
   ```

4. **Disposition: Dependent (if setup/teardown required)**:
   ```rust
   pub static RBTDLL_FIXTURE_CAPTURE_LIFECYCLE: rbtdre_Fixture = rbtdre_Fixture {
       name: RBTDRM_FIXTURE_LODE_CAPTURE_LIFECYCLE,
       disposition: rbtdre_Disposition::Dependent,  // requires per-case state (depot, etc.)
       setup: Some(rbtdll_setup_depot),             // provision GCP depot once per fixture
       teardown: Some(rbtdll_teardown_depot),       // clean up depot after all cases
       cases: RBTDLL_CASES_CAPTURE_LIFECYCLE,
   };

   fn rbtdll_setup_depot(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context) -> Result<(), String> {
       rbtdri_invoke(ctx, RBTDGC_LEVY_DEPOT, &[])
           .map(|r| if r.exit_code == 0 { Ok(()) } else { Err(format!("Levy failed: {}", r.stderr)) })?
   }
   ```

5. **Register in FIXTURES array** and wire into suite logic (where suite → fixture mapping happens, typically in engine or manifest init code)

**Fixture discovery: the `case!()` macro**
- Defined in the crate root (lib.rs or macros): `case!(function_name)` wraps a function pointer into an `rbtdre_Case` struct with metadata (name, function pointer)
- The engine calls `case_fn(&dir)` where `dir` is a temp directory scoped to the case for artifacts/logs

content.text:
Recon's back, and it gives a clean copy-adapt path — `ensconce` mirrors `enshrine` almost structurally. Here's the synthesis, and then an honest call on where to stop.

## The build, as an ordered sequence (grounded in the templates found)

**Phase 1 — bash backbone (capture-pure `ensconce`)**
1. **`rbgc_Constants.sh` + `rbgl_GarLayout.sh`** — add `RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_LODES="rbi_ld"` and `RBGL_LODES_ROOT` beside the existing four (trivial, precedent-exact). *These only land lint-clean once a consumer references them — hence they go in the same increment as the module, not before.*
2. **`rbld_Lode.sh`** (new) — `zrbld_kindle`/sentinel; the locked vocabulary (kind-letters, `rbi_` sprue, `RBLD_TAG_BASE/VOUCH`, sha256-tag former, anchor sanitizer); and `rbld_ensconce` + `zrbld_ensconce_submit` + `zrbld_ensconce_extract`, copy-adapted from the `rbfd_enshrine` family — but writing to `rbi_ld/b<stamp>` with member tags + `:rbi_vouch`, and emitting the host capture-file. Capture-pure: no rbrv.env writeback (that's the daylight guarantee — the enshrine version's `extract_anchors` rbrv writeback is exactly what we *drop*).
3. **`rbld_cli.sh`** (new) — furnish wrapper (source + kindle in dependency order), copied from `rbfd_cli.sh`.
4. **`rbgjl01-lode-ensconce.sh`** (new in-pool step) — copy-adapt `rbgje/rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh`: `skopeo inspect --raw` → measure digest → `skopeo copy` to the Lode package with member tags → push the `:rbi_vouch` envelope (oras) → `buildStepOutputs`.
5. **`rbz_zipper.sh`** — new `RBZ__GROUP_LODE "rbw-l"` group + `buz_enroll` for `rbw-lE` (ensconce) and the list/inspect/banish colophons.
6. **`tt/rbw-lE.*.sh`** (+ siblings) — the 3-line launcher tabtargets.

**Phase 2 — tests (backbone, not coverage)**
7. **`fast`**: coordinate-resolution + envelope-shape over fixtures (deterministic) — new cases + a `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_LODE_*` name in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`, registered in the `RBTDRC_FIXTURES` array.
8. **`service`**: live-GAR capture→list→inspect→delete lifecycle (`Dependent`, depot setup/teardown), asserting the mixed-artifact package round-trips and is reaped.

**Phase 3 — verify:** `rbw-tq`, then `fast` suite, then `service` against live GAR; confirm `enshrine` + conjure stay green.

## Where I'd stop, honestly

This is the natural session boundary, and I want to be straight rather than push past it: I've taken the pace from *design conversation* → *formalized paddock* → *ratified prefix* → *full integration map with an ordered build plan*, and the tree is clean (everything committed via jjx, no loose changes). What remains is a **large, exacting copy-adapt implementation** — six new files plus zipper/manifest/fixture edits — that wants a fresh context budget and the ability to build-and-test iteratively to land it lint-clean and green. Writing ~1000 lines of BCG-compliant bash by copy-adapting `rbfd` at the tail of this very long conversation would be lower-quality work that I couldn't cleanly verify here, and I'd rather not hand you that.

So my recommendation: **mount ₢BHAAA fresh for the implementation** — the plan above is the recipe, and the templates (`rbfd_enshrine`, `rbgje01`, `rbz_zipper` depot group, `rbtdrf_fast`/`rbtdrc_crucible` fixtures) are all identified.

Two ways to go from here, your call:
- **Pause** — everything's banked; resume the build as a focused mount whenever.
- **Push on now** — if you'd rather keep momentum, I'll start Phase 1 with `rbld_Lode.sh` + `rbld_cli.sh` (copy-adapt the enshrine backbone) and the `rbz_zipper` enrollment, and we accept it's a first draft to verify next session.

I won't wrap the pace — no code has landed yet. Which way do you want it?

content.text:
My recommendation: **fresh chat.** Briefly why —

- Everything load-bearing is **durably banked**: the paddock holds the locked shape + the `rbld_` ratification (committed), and the docket holds the build spec. A fresh mount re-orients against those via the mount protocol — exactly what the slate→mount handoff is designed for.
- The remaining work is **mechanical implementation that wants build-test iteration**, and this chat is thick with design dialogue that's now noise for that phase. Fresh context = better quality, lower cost, warm cache.
- The integration map's durable parts (the template files to copy-adapt) are stable entry points; only the line numbers here go stale.

The one thing worth carrying forward is the build recipe itself, so the fresh mount doesn't repeat the recon. Let me prime the docket with the template pointers (durable entry points, no line numbers) and mark the prefix ratified — then you can leave knowing the next mount starts cleanly.

input.content:
# jjezs_reslate ₢BHAAA

## Character
First kind sets the reusable shape; build with live-GAR verification. Sequential.

## Goal
Implement base-kind capture (`ensconce`) as an additive module, parallel-safe alongside live `enshrine`, per the locked Lode shape in the paddock. No cutover.

## Starting state
Gate passed — `enshrine` is cleanly separable: write path is namespace-parameterized (`RBGL_ENSHRINES_ROOT`, no hardcoded `rbi_es`), ANCHOR consumption is read-only, audit enumerators are independent per-namespace functions (a new namespace stays invisible until its own enumerator exists — see the `rbi_df` precedent), and conjure's in-pool preflight is not namespace-aware. Design locked in paddock *Lode registry layout & naming* — build to it, don't re-litigate.

## Build
- Code-module prefix is **`rbld_`** (ratified — see paddock); spec letter `RBSL` reserved.
- Capture-pure `ensconce`: resolve coordinate from the vessel ORIGIN convention -> fetch + measure digest inside Cloud Build (egress pool, never the workstation) -> push the `rbi_ld` Lode with its member tags + `:rbi_vouch` -> emit the host capture-file. Writes NO consumer config; never touches `enshrine` or ANCHOR (the enshrine rbrv.env writeback is exactly what capture-pure drops).
- Design the 1-member package so an N-member cohort slots in without restructuring (the cheap test of "cardinality is just an attribute").
- `RBSL` spec stub only if useful — do not finish it; RBS0* edits are out of scope (deferred to the remainder pace).

## Build recipe — templates to copy-adapt
`ensconce` mirrors `enshrine` structurally. Templates: the `rbfd_enshrine` orchestrator family in `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` (host orchestrate -> stitch build JSON -> extract output) and its in-pool step `rbgje/rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh`; the depot-family enrollment block in `rbz_zipper.sh` (mirror as a new `rbw-l*` Lode group); a `tt/rbw-dE.*.sh` launcher; and the `fast`/`service` fixtures in `rbtd/src/rbtdrf_fast.rs` / `rbtdrc_crucible.rs` (+ fixture-name + required-colophons in `rbtdrm_manifest.rs`, then register in the `RBTDRC_FIXTURES` array). The Lode category constant slots beside the existing four in `rbgc_Constants.sh` + `rbgl_GarLayout.sh`. For sourcing/kindle, copy a `*_cli.sh` furnish wrapper.

## Tests (backbone, not coverage)
- `fast` (deterministic): coordinate resolution, digest verification, envelope-shape over fixtures — assert capture-fidelity (recorded digest == fixture upstream digest), not existence.
- `service` (live GAR, guaranteed teardown): capture -> list -> inspect -> whole-Lode delete; assert the mixed-artifact package (member tags + `:rbi_vouch`) round-trips and is reaped. Single-call `packages delete` preferred; a GCP-run tag-delete loop is an acceptable fallback if GAR refuses.
- minimal upstream smoke — upstream is the Pale; contain its nondeterminism at one membrane.

## Done
`ensconce` captures a base into a parallel `rbi_ld` Lode with member tags + a real `:rbi_vouch` envelope + host capture-file; list / inspect / delete work against live GAR; the fast/service/smoke layers exist for base; live `enshrine` and all conjure builds remain untouched and green.
